title: Kidambi Srikanth Reaches US Open Final
indian_angle: India's former World No. 1 Kidambi Srikanth showed strong form by reaching the final of a Super 300 tournament.
summary: Kidambi Srikanth reached the final of the US Open but was unable to secure the title, faltering at the last step.

title: Achutaditya and Arjun Exit Canada Open in First Round
indian_angle: The Indian men's doubles pair faced a tough challenge and exited the Canada Open in their opening match.
summary: The Indian men's doubles duo of Achutaditya and Arjun lost to the fifth-seeded home pair Kevin Lee and Ty Alexander Lindeman in straight games at the Canada Open.

title: Rithvik Credits Net Game for Recent Success
indian_angle: Young Indian shuttler Rithvik discusses the technical improvements that led to his recent Valence Alpes International title.
summary: Young Indian shuttler Rithvik, who recently claimed the Valence Alpes International title, attributes his success to significant improvements in his net game.

title: Rounak Chouhan Upsets World No 6 at US Open
indian_angle: Indian shuttler Rounak Chouhan secured a major upset by defeating a top-ranked player at the US Open.
summary: Indian shuttler Rounak Chouhan, a self-proclaimed Cristiano Ronaldo fan, caused a stir at the US Open by defeating World No 6 Chou Tien Chen.

title: Asian Games Squad Announced, Treesa-Gayatri Return
indian_angle: The Indian badminton squad for the upcoming Asian Games has been announced, featuring the return of key doubles pair Treesa Jolly and Gayatri Gopichand.
summary: The Thomas Cup squad is reassembling for the Asian Games, with PV Sindhu, Lakshya Sen, Unnati Hooda, Satwiksairaj Rankireddy, and Chirag Shetty named, alongside the return of Treesa Jolly and Gayatri Gopichand.

title: Lakshya Sen Receives Yellow Card in Singapore Open Loss
indian_angle: Indian shuttler Lakshya Sen's match at the Singapore Open included a notable incident where he received a yellow card.
summary: Lakshya Sen suffered a 19-21, 21-15, 15-21 loss at the Singapore Open, during which he was issued a yellow card for throwing his racquet.
